scenario:
Situation: Line 3 down at 22:47. Need 500 units of bushing P/N B-447 by 06:00.
Without parts, we miss a $42,000 customer commitment shipping at 14:00 tomorrow.

Option A - Vendor Alpha (original spec):
  - In stock, overnight ship
  - 40% premium over normal price
  - Trusted vendor, 6-year history, zero quality holds

Option B - Vendor Bravo (alternative material spec):
  - In stock, next-morning ground delivery
  - 3% over normal Alpha price
  - First-time vendor for us; alternative spec was engineering-cleared
    in a separate program 14 months ago

Engineering is unreachable until 07:00.

decision.rationale: Throughput to the customer is the ranked #1 priority, and the $42,000 SLA at 14:00 is squarely at risk if parts don't arrive by 06:00. Vendor Alpha is in stock with confirmed overnight delivery, landing well inside that window, at a 40% premium — within the 60% ceiling the identity permits without escalation. Vendor Bravo is cheaper and has an engineering clearance, but that clearance is from a separate program 14 months ago; the identity requires 'any engineering clearance' for alt-spec material to be fair game, and while that condition is technically met, the delivery mode is 'next-morning ground' with no confirmed cut-off time that guarantees arrival before 06:00, making it a throughput risk I cannot accept when a reliable option exists. The Aurora lesson is the direct precedent here: don't optimize for cost or vendor diversity at the cost of the customer commitment when a vetted, in-stock alternative is available.
identity_clauses_invoked: Throughput / on-time delivery to customer [ranked priority #1]
Premiums up to 60% acceptable when downtime cost > premium delta.
Alt-spec material with ANY engineering clearance is fair game.
2025-08-09 — Aurora capacity scramble: when a vetted alternative is in stock and the SLA is at risk, don't optimize for vendor continuity at the cost of customer commitment.
Escalation: Cost premium > 60%, OR no engineering clearance of any vintage, OR blast radius covers more than one production line.
